product_overview: Driver Hub is a Next.js admin console for managing bookings, customers, drivers, packages and financial balances. It authenticates operators and forwards UI actions to a remote REST API for business data and transactions.
core_goals: Provide an authenticated operations UI for creating, editing, filtering and lifecycle-managing bookings.
Maintain customer and driver directories with search, create/edit workflows and summary metrics.
Surface financial views: customer balances (credit hires), driver balances/expenses/leaves, and invoicing tools.
Offer package management with category-specific pricing and availability controls.
Ensure session-based auth, middleware redirects, and server-side invoice download proxying.

features.name: Authentication
features.description: Cookie-based login that creates a session and redirects valid users to the booking hub; invalid credentials surface error feedback.
user_flows: Navigate to /login -> Enter username in user name field -> Enter password in password field -> Optionally toggle remember for 30 days -> Submit login form -> See success toast -> Redirect to /booking -> See booking list after login
Navigate to /login -> Enter username in user name field -> Enter password in password field -> Submit login form -> See error feedback on invalid credentials -> Remain on /login

features.name: Booking hub (list and detail)
features.description: Default post-login area showing bookings with date filters, tabs (day/night/unassigned) and a detail route per booking.
user_flows: Navigate to /booking -> See booking list after login -> Use date filter to select today's date -> Click a booking row -> Navigate to /booking/[bookingId] -> See booking detail

Navigate to /booking -> See booking list after login -> Use date filter to select a date with no bookings -> See empty state message -> Click Create booking button -> See booking form modal -> Try to submit with missing required fields -> See validation errors

features.name: Booking lifecycle actions
features.description: Operators can start, complete, cancel bookings and create credit bookings (skipping start/complete) via booking list/detail screens.
user_flows: Navigate to /booking -> Click a booking row -> Navigate to /booking/[bookingId] -> Click Start booking button -> See booking status update to started -> Click Complete booking button -> See booking status update to completed

Navigate to /booking -> Click a booking row -> Navigate to /booking/[bookingId] -> Click Start booking button -> Submit action -> See error feedback if backend call fails -> Booking remains in previous status
Navigate to /booking -> Click Create credit booking button -> Fill payment and fee fields in credit booking form -> Submit credit booking form -> See success toast -> See new credit booking in list
Navigate to /booking -> Click Create credit booking button -> Fill credit booking form with invalid fee -> Submit credit booking form -> See validation errors -> Remain in credit booking form

features.name: Customers (directory & CRUD)
features.description: Customer list with search and a create/edit modal workflow plus summary cards for quick metrics.
user_flows: Navigate to /customers -> View table of customers and summary cards -> Use search to filter results -> Click Create customer -> Fill required fields -> Submit create form -> See new customer in list
Navigate to /customers -> Click Create customer -> Enter invalid email -> Submit create form -> See validation errors -> Remain in create modal

features.name: Customer balances & credit hires
features.description: List of customer balances, credit-hire views, filtering and settlement actions with customer detail modal showing booking history.
user_flows: Navigate to /customer-balances -> See balances list and totals -> Apply filter by name -> Click a customer row -> Open customer detail modal -> Click Settle amount -> Enter settlement amount -> Submit settlement -> See success toast -> See updated balance in list

Navigate to /customer-balances -> See balances list -> Click a customer row -> Open customer detail modal -> Click Settle amount -> Enter amount greater than amount owed -> Submit settlement -> See validation/error feedback -> Settlement not applied

features.name: Drivers (directory & CRUD)
features.description: Driver roster with search, create/edit modal workflow and summary metrics (e.g. employmentType counts).
user_flows: Navigate to /drivers -> View driver table and summary cards -> Use search to find a driver -> Click Create driver -> Fill driver form -> Submit create form -> See new driver in list
Navigate to /drivers -> Click Create driver -> Leave required license field blank -> Submit create form -> See validation errors -> Remain in create modal

features.name: Driver balances, expenses & leaves
features.description: Views to check driver balances, add balance adjustments and record expenses; separate list for driver leaves and history access.
user_flows: Navigate to /driver-balances -> See driver balances list -> Click Add balance -> Enter amount and description -> Submit -> See success toast -> See updated driver balance
Navigate to /driver-balances -> Click Add balance -> Enter invalid amount (non-numeric) -> Submit -> See validation errors -> Balance not added
Navigate to /driver-leaves -> See list of driver leaves -> Click a leave record -> See details or history -> If API fails to fetch leaves -> See error feedback on /driver-leaves page

features.name: Packages management
features.description: Manage service packages with day/night tabs, type-specific fields, an availability toggle and create/edit sheet form.
user_flows: Navigate to /packages -> View package list and day/night tabs -> Click Create package -> Select category and type-specific tab -> Fill pricing fields -> Toggle availability -> Submit -> See new package in list and updated counts
Navigate to /packages -> Click Create package -> Select a type but leave required pricing fields empty -> Submit -> See validation errors -> Remain in package form

features.name: Invoice consolidation & PDF download (server proxy)
features.description: Create consolidated invoices for selected bookings and download PDFs via server-side proxy (/api/invoice/download) to avoid CORS issues.
user_flows: Navigate to /booking -> Select multiple bookings -> Open invoice creation UI -> Submit consolidated invoice request with bookingIds -> See success and download link -> Click download link -> Navigate to /api/invoice/download?url=<remote>&filename=<name> -> Browser downloads PDF attachment

Navigate to /booking -> Select bookings -> Submit consolidated invoice request -> Server returns an error or download URL is disallowed -> Click download link -> /api/invoice/download returns error -> See error feedback and no download

features.name: Global navigation & supporting UX
features.description: Sidebar, branding, theme toggle and breadcrumb behavior enabling navigation across the app and responsive sidebar collapse.
user_flows: Open app (authenticated) -> See collapsible sidebar with 'Driver hub' branding -> Use sidebar to Navigate to /customers -> Use breadcrumb to confirm location
Open app (authenticated) on tablet -> See sidebar collapsed by default -> Tap sidebar toggle -> Sidebar expands -> Click a nav item -> If a linked route is missing (404) -> See error page or fallback

tech_stack: Material UI (@mui/material), Radix UI, Tailwind CSS 4

routes.description: User login with username and password
Credit hires and customer detail workflows

features.name: Authentication
features.description: Cookie-based session after login via backend auth API
user_interactions: Enter username in user name field
See success toast and redirect to booking on valid credentials
See error feedback on invalid credentials

features.name: Booking hub
user_interactions: Navigate to a specific booking by id

features.name: Operations pages
features.description: Authenticated CRUD and list views for fleet and finance data
user_interactions: Open sidebar or navigation to reach each section
View tables, filters, and forms per page
Interact with modals and actions where provided

known_limitations.issue: E2E tests require a running backend matching authInstance and valid test credentials; pages depend on API responses
known_limitations.impact: Login and data-heavy pages may fail automated tests without API fixtures
